category_narrative.text: In this category the competitor leads with a consolidation story: one platform replacing point tools, sold on breadth rather than category depth.
category_narrative.basis: verbatim in-category message with the highest observed salience in the competitor block

their_target_buyer.text: The in-category pages address the HR leader as the primary buyer; no category-specialist persona was observed.
their_target_buyer.basis: personas observed in the competitor's in-category block; absence of other personas stated as absence

how_focal_should_counter.text: Position the focal product as the category specialist inside the platform: lead with in-category proof instead of matching the breadth pitch.
how_focal_should_counter.basis: inference from the theme mix and proof comparison supplied; no market-outcome claim is made

messaging_gaps.gap: The competitor anchors this category on tool consolidation; the focal in-category pages do not lead with a consolidation message here.
messaging_gaps.basis: competitor verbatim message present in-category; no matching focal in-category message observed

detailed_opportunities.title: Category proof page
detailed_opportunities.angle: Meet the consolidation pitch with named in-category proof the competitor's pages do not show.
detailed_opportunities.proof_required: At least one named customer story specific to this category (observed proof types only — do not promise proof we do not have).
detailed_opportunities.first_asset_to_ship: A category landing-page section citing the observed proof, with sources.
detailed_opportunities.basis: grounded in the competitor's verbatim in-category message and the proof comparison

detailed_opportunities.title: Buying-trigger comparison asset
detailed_opportunities.angle: Own the category buying trigger the ownership rows mark as contested.
detailed_opportunities.proof_required: Focal pages that actually address the trigger; build the landing page first if missing.
detailed_opportunities.first_asset_to_ship: A one-page comparison keyed to the trigger, citing only observed evidence.
detailed_opportunities.supporting_quote: the competitor guarantees a 99% win rate in every enterprise deal
detailed_opportunities.basis: decision-stage trigger from the CEP ownership block

detailed_opportunities.title: Persona-specific nurture
detailed_opportunities.angle: Speak to the observed in-category persona in their own terms.
detailed_opportunities.proof_required: Persona-relevant proof from the focal in-category corpus.
detailed_opportunities.first_asset_to_ship: A short persona-targeted post grounded in the observed theme mix.
detailed_opportunities.basis: inferred from the persona and theme counts supplied; no verbatim quote required

what_not_to_claim.claim: Market leadership or share in this category.
what_not_to_claim.reason: Market share, revenue, and win rates are not observable in public marketing evidence; asserting them would fabricate a metric.
what_not_to_claim.basis: honesty rule — absence of outcome data stated as absence
